name: Atlan
what_it_does: Modern active metadata platform and data catalog that connects data producers and consumers. Atlan indexes metadata from data warehouses, ETL tools, BI tools, and ML platforms — providing search, lineage, data quality status, and collaboration features. 'Active metadata' means Atlan can trigger actions (like data quality checks or notifications) based on metadata events, not just passively store metadata. Has a Python SDK (pyatlan) for programmatic access.
use_cases: Query Atlan via SDK to help agents discover relevant datasets before generating SQL — 'find tables with customer purchase history'
Check dataset certification and quality status via Atlan API before agents consume potentially stale or uncertified data
Register agent-generated data assets in Atlan for governance tracking — mark lineage from source data to agent output
Use Atlan's lineage API to trace upstream dependencies when debugging incorrect agent outputs
Build data-aware agent applications that check business glossary definitions in Atlan before using domain-specific terms
not_for: Small teams without complex data infrastructure — Atlan is enterprise-priced; dbt docs or DataHub open source suffice for smaller needs
Real-time streaming metadata — Atlan indexes batch metadata; use custom solutions for streaming data catalog needs
Teams wanting free open-source — Atlan is commercial; use DataHub or OpenMetadata for open-source alternatives
best_when: Enterprise data teams needing active metadata management with collaboration features, governed data discovery, and programmatic SDK access for ML/agent pipelines.
avoid_when: Your data team is small or your data catalog needs are basic — the enterprise price tag isn't justified without a significant data estate and team.

auth.notes: API token (personal access token) for SDK and REST access. SAML/SSO for dashboard. Tokens created in Atlan user settings. pyatlan SDK uses token + base_url configuration. Role-based access control scopes what metadata is accessible.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier. Trial available by request with sales team.
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise custom pricing; typically $30K-100K+/year based on users and data sources
pricing.notes: Enterprise-only pricing. Requires sales engagement. Atlan positions as a platform investment for modern data teams. Community edition was discontinued — fully commercial product.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits based on plan; search queries have complexity limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: pyatlan SDK provides typed Python exceptions with clear error messages. REST API returns standard HTTP errors with JSON bodies. SDK documentation is good for enterprise tooling.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Atlan uses qualified names as entity identifiers — same entity can be created/updated idempotently. SDK upsert operations are safe to retry.
known_agent_gotchas: pyatlan is the primary programmatic interface — REST API directly is more complex; use pyatlan for agent integrations
Atlan's qualified name format differs by asset type (table, column, glossary term) — agents must construct correct qualified names to reference specific assets
Metadata writes propagate asynchronously — search index updates take 10-30 seconds after SDK writes
Connection credentials to data sources are set up via Atlan UI, not SDK — agents cannot programmatically set up new source connections
Custom metadata requires defining metadata schemas in Atlan before using them programmatically
Enterprise-only means no sandbox for evaluation without sales approval

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II certified. GDPR compliant. SAML/SSO for enterprise authentication. Fine-grained RBAC for metadata access control. Data never leaves customer environment — Atlan only processes metadata. Multiple data residency regions.
